title: A Puerto Rico Government Agency Exposed 1 Million Social Security Numbers
excerpt: A cybersecurity loophole in an official government mapping service left private data easily accessible, Centro de Periodismo Investigativo and ProPublica learned.
content_html:
The government agency that collects property taxes in Puerto Rico inadvertently exposed the Social Security numbers of approximately 1 million people, Centro de Periodismo Investigativo and ProPublica learned.

It was the latest cybersecurity lapse for the Puerto Rico government, which in the past three years has seen technology breaches interrupt government services, take websites offline and lead to citizens’ personal information being published on the dark web.

CPI and ProPublica became aware of the vulnerability related to the Municipal Revenue Collection Center’s interactive property map, known as the Catastro Digital, and notified the agency in mid-June.

The online tool provides information, such as size, boundaries, tax assessment, sale price and owner’s name, for every registered property on the island.

While a simple search of the map wouldn’t reveal sensitive information, anyone who understands how websites request data could download unprotected personal information such as Social Security numbers without a username or password.

The news organizations were able to verify the security hole and provided the agency, known by its Spanish initials, CRIM, with a detailed description of the issue that included the specific server and folders that contained the compromised data.

Despite the notification, CRIM has repeatedly denied there were any problems with its system.

“Following a review of the Catastro Digital platform, it was determined that there was NO breach of confidential personal taxpayer information, as the Catastro Digital does NOT contain or display the type of information alluded to,” CRIM Executive Director Javier García Cintrón said.

But a few days after CPI and ProPublica contacted CRIM, the news organizations were able to see that the security holes had been patched.

García denied that, saying there was no need to fix any problem. A Puerto Rico law requires any entity, including government agencies, to promptly notify users if their personal information has been breached. But García said the agency would not reach out to users to tell them that their Social Security numbers were potentially exposed, as “no protected information was at risk.”

CRIM also did not notify the Puerto Rico Innovation & Technology Service, known as PRITS, which oversees all government information technology systems. The government’s cybersecurity protocol requires informing PRITS of “any suspected security incident.”

A PRITS spokesperson declined to answer questions and said they had to be submitted under Puerto Rico’s public information law, which is meant to allow citizens to get government records and not to answer press questions.

So far this year, more than 2 million attempted cyberattacks have been recorded within the Puerto Rico government, PRITS data shows. Half of these were deemed critical incidents, which involve “severe impact on critical operations, the compromise of sensitive data, or an imminent threat to agency security or government data,” according to the agency.

In March, citizens saw their driver’s license and registration appointments postponed after an attempted cyberattack on Transportation Department systems. Last year, Puerto Rico residents could not verify their criminal record status, which they need for employment, for almost a week because of an “unauthorized access” to the local Justice Department’s criminal records database. In 2023, Puerto Rico water utility clients and employees saw their personal information published on the dark web after a ransomware attack.

An increase in attacks prompted Puerto Rico lawmakers in 2024 to approve a comprehensive cybersecurity law, Act 40, which mandated all government agencies implement minimum cybersecurity standards and principles. It also established penalties for noncompliance and required all government agencies to conduct a risk assessment at least once annually.

But three cybersecurity experts said agencies have failed to fully implement the security standards set out under the law, even as attacks become more frequent and sophisticated. Instead of periodically assessing and tackling vulnerabilities that prevent these attacks, agencies are reactive, they said.

A Puerto Rico Inspector General Office report released late last year found deficiencies across 90 local government agencies, with 60% of them failing to conduct vulnerability assessments of their IT systems.

The government would be in “much better shape” if it focused on employee training and implemented tools like multifactor authentication on the front end, said Carlos Pérez, a cybersecurity expert in Puerto Rico who is director of security intelligence at TrustedSec, a company that consults with governments and private companies.

“We are addressing the symptom but not the disease,” he said.

In most cases, the cybersecurity law falls short of requiring unified standards across the government, said a former government IT employee, who asked not be named because he feared professional repercussions. That lack of a single set of standards has allowed agencies to decide on their own how they protect personal data.

García explained that, as part of CRIM’s security measures, the agency uses passwords, usernames and text messages to validate identity. He denied that anyone could access the Catastro Digital database without a password except to conduct individual searches through the public website.

The ability to access Social Security numbers through CRIM’s property map raises concerns given the proliferation of private companies that sell Puerto Rico real estate information, obtained from public databases such as Catastro Digital. Any of those companies could have accessed the data, including personal information.

At least three property listings companies contacted by CPI and ProPublica said they were not aware of any vulnerability and did not access the sensitive data.

A Puerto Rico law requires any entity, including government agencies, to promptly notify users if their personal information has been breached. But García said the agency would not reach out to users to tell them that their Social Security numbers were potentially exposed, as “no protected information was at risk.” CRIM also did not notify the Puerto Rico Innovation & Technology Service, known as PRITS, which oversees all government information technology systems. The government’s cybersecurity protocol requires informing PRITS of “any suspected security incident.” A PRITS spokesperson declined to answer questions and said they had to be submitted under Puerto Rico’s public information law, which is meant to allow citizens to get government records and not to answer press questions. sentences.zh: CRIM 也没有通知波多黎各创新与技术服务机构（PRITS），该机构负责监督所有政府信息技术系统。

sentences.en: The government’s cybersecurity protocol requires informing PRITS of “any suspected security incident.”
sentences.zh: 政府的网络安全协议要求向 PRITS 通报“任何可疑的安全事件”。

blocks.html_en: A PRITS spokesperson declined to answer questions and said they had to be submitted under Puerto Rico’s public information law, which is meant to allow citizens to get government records and not to answer press questions.

sentences.zh: PRITS 发言人拒绝回答问题，并表示这些问题必须根据波多黎各的公共信息法提交，该法旨在允许公民获取政府记录而不是回答媒体问题。

blocks.html_en: So far this year, more than 2 million attempted cyberattacks have been recorded within the Puerto Rico government, PRITS data shows. Half of these were deemed critical incidents, which involve “severe impact on critical operations, the compromise of sensitive data, or an imminent threat to agency security or government data,” according to the agency.

sentences.en: So far this year, more than 2 million attempted cyberattacks have been recorded within the Puerto Rico government, PRITS data shows.
sentences.zh: PRITS 数据显示，今年到目前为止，波多黎各政府内部已记录了超过 200 万次未遂网络攻击。

sentences.en: Half of these were deemed critical incidents, which involve “severe impact on critical operations, the compromise of sensitive data, or an imminent threat to agency security or government data,” according to the agency.
sentences.zh: 该机构表示，其中一半被视为严重事件，涉及“对关键操作的严重影响、敏感数据的泄露或对机构安全或政府数据的迫在眉睫的威胁”。

blocks.html_en: In March, citizens saw their driver’s license and registration appointments postponed after an attempted cyberattack on Transportation Department systems. Last year, Puerto Rico residents could not verify their criminal record status, which they need for employment, for almost a week because of an “unauthorized access” to the local Justice Department’s criminal records database. In 2023, Puerto Rico water utility clients and employees saw their personal information published on the dark web after a ransomware attack.

sentences.en: In March, citizens saw their driver’s license and registration appointments postponed after an attempted cyberattack on Transportation Department systems.
sentences.zh: 今年三月，在交通部系统遭到企图网络攻击后，公民的驾驶执照和登记预约被推迟。

sentences.en: Last year, Puerto Rico residents could not verify their criminal record status, which they need for employment, for almost a week because of an “unauthorized access” to the local Justice Department’s criminal records database.
sentences.zh: 去年，由于当地司法部的犯罪记录数据库“未经授权访问”，波多黎各居民近一周无法核实他们就业所需的犯罪记录状况。

sentences.en: In 2023, Puerto Rico water utility clients and employees saw their personal information published on the dark web after a ransomware attack.
sentences.zh: 2023 年，波多黎各自来水公司的客户和员工在勒索软件攻击后看到他们的个人信息被发布在暗网上。

blocks.html_en: An increase in attacks prompted Puerto Rico lawmakers in 2024 to approve a comprehensive cybersecurity law, Act 40, which mandated all government agencies implement minimum cybersecurity standards and principles. It also established penalties for noncompliance and required all government agencies to conduct a risk assessment at least once annually.

sentences.en: An increase in attacks prompted Puerto Rico lawmakers in 2024 to approve a comprehensive cybersecurity law, Act 40, which mandated all government agencies implement minimum cybersecurity standards and principles.
sentences.zh: 攻击的增加促使波多黎各立法者于 2024 年批准了一项全面的网络安全法，即第 40 号法案，该法案要求所有政府机构实施最低网络安全标准和原则。

sentences.en: It also established penalties for noncompliance and required all government agencies to conduct a risk assessment at least once annually.
sentences.zh: 它还规定了对违规行为的处罚，并要求所有政府机构每年至少进行一次风险评估。

blocks.html_en: But three cybersecurity experts said agencies have failed to fully implement the security standards set out under the law, even as attacks become more frequent and sophisticated. Instead of periodically assessing and tackling vulnerabilities that prevent these attacks, agencies are reactive, they said.

sentences.en: But three cybersecurity experts said agencies have failed to fully implement the security standards set out under the law, even as attacks become more frequent and sophisticated.
sentences.zh: 但三名网络安全专家表示，即使攻击变得更加频繁和复杂，各机构仍未能充分执行法律规定的安全标准。

sentences.en: Instead of periodically assessing and tackling vulnerabilities that prevent these attacks, agencies are reactive, they said.
sentences.zh: 他们表示，各机构没有定期评估和解决防止这些攻击的漏洞，而是采取被动反应。

blocks.html_en: A Puerto Rico Inspector General Office report released late last year found deficiencies across 90 local government agencies, with 60% of them failing to conduct vulnerability assessments of their IT systems.

sentences.en: A Puerto Rico Inspector General Office report released late last year found deficiencies across 90 local government agencies, with 60% of them failing to conduct vulnerability assessments of their IT systems.
sentences.zh: 波多黎各监察长办公室去年年底发布的一份报告发现，90 个地方政府机构存在缺陷，其中 60% 未能对其 IT 系统进行漏洞评估。

blocks.html_en: The government would be in “much better shape” if it focused on employee training and implemented tools like multifactor authentication on the front end, said Carlos Pérez, a cybersecurity expert in Puerto Rico who is director of security intelligence at TrustedSec, a company that consults with governments and private companies.

sentences.en: The government would be in “much better shape” if it focused on employee training and implemented tools like multifactor authentication on the front end, said Carlos Pérez, a cybersecurity expert in Puerto Rico who is director of security intelligence at TrustedSec, a company that consults with governments and private companies.
sentences.zh: 波多黎各网络安全专家卡洛斯·佩雷斯 (Carlos Pérez) 表示，如果政府注重员工培训并在前端实施多因素身份验证等工具，政府的状况将会“好得多”。TrustedSec 是一家为政府和私营公司提供咨询的公司。

blocks.html_en: “We are addressing the symptom but not the disease,” he said.

sentences.en: “We are addressing the symptom but not the disease,” he said.
sentences.zh: “我们正在解决症状，而不是疾病，”他说。

blocks.html_en: In most cases, the cybersecurity law falls short of requiring unified standards across the government, said a former government IT employee, who asked not be named because he feared professional repercussions. That lack of a single set of standards has allowed agencies to decide on their own how they protect personal data.

sentences.en: In most cases, the cybersecurity law falls short of requiring unified standards across the government, said a former government IT employee, who asked not be named because he feared professional repercussions.
sentences.zh: 一位前政府 IT 员工表示，在大多数情况下，网络安全法没有要求政府统一标准。由于担心职业影响，他要求不透露姓名。

sentences.en: That lack of a single set of standards has allowed agencies to decide on their own how they protect personal data.
sentences.zh: 由于缺乏一套统一的标准，各机构可以自行决定如何保护个人数据。

blocks.html_en: García explained that, as part of CRIM’s security measures, the agency uses passwords, usernames and text messages to validate identity. He denied that anyone could access the Catastro Digital database without a password except to conduct individual searches through the public website.

sentences.en: García explained that, as part of CRIM’s security measures, the agency uses passwords, usernames and text messages to validate identity.
sentences.zh: 加西亚解释说，作为 CRIM 安全措施的一部分，该机构使用密码、用户名和短信来验证身份。

sentences.en: He denied that anyone could access the Catastro Digital database without a password except to conduct individual searches through the public website.
sentences.zh: 他否认任何人都可以在没有密码的情况下访问 Catastro Digital 数据库，除非通过公共网站进行个人搜索。

blocks.html_en: The ability to access Social Security numbers through CRIM’s property map raises concerns given the proliferation of private companies that sell Puerto Rico real estate information, obtained from public databases such as Catastro Digital. Any of those companies could have accessed the data, including personal information.

sentences.en: The ability to access Social Security numbers through CRIM’s property map raises concerns given the proliferation of private companies that sell Puerto Rico real estate information, obtained from public databases such as Catastro Digital.
sentences.zh: 鉴于出售波多黎各房地产信息（从 Catastro Digital 等公共数据库获得）的私营公司数量激增，通过 CRIM 的房地产地图获取社会安全号码的能力引起了人们的担忧。

sentences.en: Any of those companies could have accessed the data, including personal information.
sentences.zh: 这些公司中的任何一家都可以访问这些数据，包括个人信息。

blocks.html_en: At least three property listings companies contacted by CPI and ProPublica said they were not aware of any vulnerability and did not access the sensitive data.

sentences.en: At least three property listings companies contacted by CPI and ProPublica said they were not aware of any vulnerability and did not access the sensitive data.
sentences.zh: CPI 和 ProPublica 联系的至少三家房地产上市公司表示，他们不知道有任何漏洞，也没有访问敏感数据。

content_html_zh:
Centro de periodismo Investigativo 和 ProPublica 获悉，波多黎各征收财产税的政府机构无意中泄露了约 100 万人的社会保障号码。

这是波多黎各政府最新的网络安全漏洞，在过去三年中，技术漏洞导致政府服务中断、网站离线并导致公民个人信息在暗网上发布。

CPI 和 ProPublica 意识到与市政税收中心的交互式财产地图（称为 Catastro Digital）相关的漏洞，并于 6 月中旬通知了该机构。

该在线工具提供岛上每处注册房产的信息，例如面积、边界、税收评估、销售价格和业主姓名。

虽然简单的地图搜索不会泄露敏感信息，但任何了解网站如何请求数据的人都可以下载不受保护的个人信息，例如无需用户名或密码的社会安全号码。

这些新闻机构能够验证该安全漏洞，并向该机构（其西班牙语缩写 CRIM）提供了问题的详细描述，其中包括包含受损数据的特定服务器和文件夹。

尽管收到了通知，CRIM 一再否认其系统存在任何问题。

CRIM 执行董事哈维尔·加西亚·辛特龙 (Javier García Cintrón) 表示：“对 Catastro Digital 平台进行审查后，确定不存在泄露个人纳税人机密信息的情况，因为 Catastro Digital 不包含或显示所提到的信息类型。”

但在 CPI 和 ProPublica 联系 CRIM 几天后，新闻机构发现安全漏洞已被修补。

加西亚对此予以否认，称没有必要解决任何问题。 波多黎各法律要求任何实体，包括政府机构，如果用户的个人信息遭到泄露，必须立即通知用户。 但加西亚表示，该机构不会联系用户，告诉他们他们的社会安全号码可能会暴露，因为“没有受保护的信息面临风险”。

CRIM 也没有通知波多黎各创新与技术服务机构（PRITS），该机构负责监督所有政府信息技术系统。 政府的网络安全协议要求向 PRITS 通报“任何可疑的安全事件”。

PRITS 发言人拒绝回答问题，并表示这些问题必须根据波多黎各的公共信息法提交，该法旨在允许公民获取政府记录而不是回答媒体问题。

PRITS 数据显示，今年到目前为止，波多黎各政府内部已记录了超过 200 万次未遂网络攻击。 该机构表示，其中一半被视为严重事件，涉及“对关键操作的严重影响、敏感数据的泄露或对机构安全或政府数据的迫在眉睫的威胁”。

今年三月，在交通部系统遭到企图网络攻击后，公民的驾驶执照和登记预约被推迟。 去年，由于当地司法部的犯罪记录数据库“未经授权访问”，波多黎各居民近一周无法核实他们就业所需的犯罪记录状况。 2023 年，波多黎各自来水公司的客户和员工在勒索软件攻击后看到他们的个人信息被发布在暗网上。

攻击的增加促使波多黎各立法者于 2024 年批准了一项全面的网络安全法，即第 40 号法案，该法案要求所有政府机构实施最低网络安全标准和原则。 它还规定了对违规行为的处罚，并要求所有政府机构每年至少进行一次风险评估。

但三名网络安全专家表示，即使攻击变得更加频繁和复杂，各机构仍未能充分执行法律规定的安全标准。 他们表示，各机构没有定期评估和解决防止这些攻击的漏洞，而是采取被动反应。

波多黎各监察长办公室去年年底发布的一份报告发现，90 个地方政府机构存在缺陷，其中 60% 未能对其 IT 系统进行漏洞评估。

波多黎各网络安全专家卡洛斯·佩雷斯 (Carlos Pérez) 表示，如果政府注重员工培训并在前端实施多因素身份验证等工具，政府的状况将会“好得多”。TrustedSec 是一家为政府和私营公司提供咨询的公司。

“我们正在解决症状，而不是疾病，”他说。

一位前政府 IT 员工表示，在大多数情况下，网络安全法没有要求政府统一标准。由于担心职业影响，他要求不透露姓名。 由于缺乏一套统一的标准，各机构可以自行决定如何保护个人数据。

加西亚解释说，作为 CRIM 安全措施的一部分，该机构使用密码、用户名和短信来验证身份。 他否认任何人都可以在没有密码的情况下访问 Catastro Digital 数据库，除非通过公共网站进行个人搜索。

鉴于出售波多黎各房地产信息（从 Catastro Digital 等公共数据库获得）的私营公司数量激增，通过 CRIM 的房地产地图获取社会安全号码的能力引起了人们的担忧。 这些公司中的任何一家都可以访问这些数据，包括个人信息。

CPI 和 ProPublica 联系的至少三家房地产上市公司表示，他们不知道有任何漏洞，也没有访问敏感数据。
